title: Alien: Romulus Projections Tease Surprising Opening Weekend at Box Office
text: The latest installment in the sci-fi horror franchise is projected to achieve remarkable box office performance. Industry forecasts suggest the film could earn between "$35 million to $50 million" domestically in its opening weekend, potentially setting a franchise record. Currently, 2012's "Prometheus" holds the best opening weekend for an "Alien" film at $51 million. Director Fede Álvarez's "Alien: Romulus", starring Cailee Spaeny, could surpass this mark if it reaches the upper end of predictions. The article provides a comprehensive breakdown of opening weekends for all nine "Alien" movies, ranging from the original 1979 film's $3.5 million to "Prometheus'" $51 million. It notes that "Alien: Covenant" (2017) earned $36.2 million in its debut. The piece also examines lifetime domestic box office totals, with "Prometheus" leading at $126.5 million. Globally, "Prometheus" remains the franchise's highest earner at $403.4 million, though no "Alien" film has exceeded $500 million worldwide. The article mentions potential competition from "Deadpool & Wolverine" and confirms the film's theatrical release date as August 16, 2024.
